taskDescription: Click on the key biochemistry and clinical terms related to organ function tests and hormones.
textField:
The liver function test panel includes *ALT* (*SGPT*), *AST* (*SGOT*), *ALP* (alkaline *phosphatase*), *GGT*, *bilirubin*, *albumin*, and *prothrombin* time. *ALT* is the most specific marker of *hepatocellular* injury as it is a *cytoplasmic* enzyme found almost exclusively in *hepatocytes*.

An *AST* to ALT ratio greater than 2:1 suggests *alcoholic* *hepatitis*, because alcohol depletes *pyridoxal* *phosphate* (vitamin B6). Elevated *ALP* with raised *GGT* indicates *cholestatic* liver disease. Serum *albumin* reflects hepatic *synthetic* function with a half-life of approximately 21 days.

In primary *hypothyroidism*, *TSH* levels are *elevated* while free *T4* levels are *decreased*. Serum *creatinine* is the standard marker of *glomerular* *filtration* rate. *Cystatin* C is an emerging alternative marker that is less affected by muscle mass.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the key terms of organ function tests.
Good effort! You identified many key terms.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of the terminology.
